title: rubus × agrestis subsp. leucophaeus
intro: Rubus × agrestis subsp. leucophaeus, also known as the white-stemmed wild bramble, is a subspecies of wild raspberry that is native to Western Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Rubus × agrestis subsp. leucophaeus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its potential health benefits.
